codes.message: The workload must contain at least one task.
codes.recovery: Add one bounded task before admission.

codes.message: The workload exceeds the task capacity.
codes.recovery: Remove tasks or select a deliberately larger task capacity.

codes.message: Each task identity must be unique.
codes.recovery: Give every admitted task a distinct identity.

codes.message: A task deadline or period is invalid.
codes.recovery: Use a positive period and a deadline no greater than that period.

codes.message: Task jitter must be below its deadline.
codes.recovery: Reduce the jitter bound or increase the admitted deadline.

codes.message: A task execution bound is missing or too large.
codes.recovery: Provide a positive execution bound that fits the deadline.

codes.message: Task execution plus blocking exceeds its deadline.
codes.recovery: Reduce execution or blocking, or increase the admitted deadline.

codes.message: The workload utilization exceeds one core.
codes.recovery: Reduce execution budgets or task rates.

codes.message: Response-time analysis found a missed deadline.
codes.recovery: Reduce interference, execution, or blocking for the named task.

codes.message: The workload exceeds its flash profile.
codes.recovery: Disable optional features or select a measured larger flash profile.

codes.message: The workload exceeds its RAM profile.
codes.recovery: Reduce static allocations or select a measured larger RAM profile.

codes.message: The workload exceeds its sample-pool profile.
codes.recovery: Reduce retained samples or select a larger bounded pool.

codes.message: Admission arithmetic overflowed.
codes.recovery: Reduce the declared bounds and check their units.

codes.message: The wake-latency bound exceeds a task deadline.
codes.recovery: Reduce the wake bound or increase the affected deadline.

codes.message: A task phase must be below its period.
codes.recovery: Choose a phase from zero through period minus one.

codes.message: An interrupt priority is outside the target range.
codes.recovery: Select a priority implemented by the target.

codes.message: An interrupt priority is reserved by the platform stack.
codes.recovery: Use an application priority not reserved by the selected stack.

codes.message: An interrupt timing or stack contract is invalid.
codes.recovery: Provide positive bounded timing and stack values.

codes.message: An interrupt step requests an unbounded operation.
codes.recovery: Move the operation out of interrupt context or use a bounded variant.

codes.message: The nested interrupt-stack budget is exceeded.
codes.recovery: Reduce nesting or stack use, or select a measured larger bound.

codes.message: Interrupt interference exceeds a deadline.
codes.recovery: Reduce higher-priority interrupt work or relax the affected deadline.

codes.message: Workload must contain a non-empty task list.
codes.recovery: Add the kernel task and at least one application task.

codes.message: Every task needs one stable lowercase name.
codes.recovery: Use a name matching [a-z][a-z0-9_-]{0,47}.

codes.message: Task names must be unique.
codes.recovery: Rename the duplicate task.

codes.message: Every workload needs the kernel task.
codes.recovery: Add the kernel task to the workload.

codes.message: Task criticality must be one of the known roles.
codes.recovery: Choose a role accepted by the workload schema.

codes.message: Resource and timing numbers must be non-negative.
codes.recovery: Replace negative values with measured non-negative bounds.

codes.message: A task budget must fit inside its period.
codes.recovery: Reduce the budget or increase the period.

codes.message: Task dependencies must be a short unique list.
codes.recovery: Remove duplicate or excessive dependency names.

codes.message: Task dependencies must name existing tasks.
codes.recovery: Correct or add the named dependency task.

codes.message: The kernel starts first and cannot depend on app tasks.
codes.recovery: Remove dependencies from the kernel task.

codes.message: Each wire must be written as [from, to].
codes.recovery: Provide exactly one source and one destination.

codes.message: Wire endpoints must name existing tasks.
codes.recovery: Correct or add each endpoint task.

codes.message: Startup dependencies cannot form a cycle.
codes.recovery: Remove one dependency edge from the cycle.

codes.message: Features must be one object of boolean switches.
codes.recovery: Use one features object with true or false values.

codes.message: Feature target is unsupported.
codes.recovery: Choose a target present in the feature catalog.

codes.message: Feature name is unknown for this target.
codes.recovery: Choose a feature listed for the selected target.

codes.message: Feature values must match the catalog.
codes.recovery: Use the value type declared by the feature catalog.

codes.message: Feature is unavailable for this target.
codes.recovery: Disable it or choose a target with linked evidence.

codes.message: Enabled features conflict.
codes.recovery: Disable one of the named conflicting features.

codes.message: Workload schema version is unsupported.
codes.recovery: Migrate the document to the current workload schema.

codes.message: Application graph state does not allow this operation.
codes.recovery: Declare tasks and wires before running the graph.

codes.message: Names must use stable lowercase labels.
codes.recovery: Use a name matching [a-z][a-z0-9_-]{0,47}.

codes.message: Task rate and period must be valid.
codes.recovery: Use a positive period no longer than the wrap-safe interval.

codes.message: Application task capacity is exceeded.
codes.recovery: Remove tasks or deliberately select a larger bounded capacity.

codes.message: Application wire capacity is exceeded.
codes.recovery: Use a capacity from 1 through 64 or declare fewer wires.

codes.message: Wire endpoints must name existing tasks.
codes.recovery: Declare both endpoint tasks before the wire.

codes.message: Task name is already declared.
codes.recovery: Rename or remove the duplicate task.

codes.message: Task timing or resource options are invalid.
codes.recovery: Keep phase, deadline, budget, blocking, and memory within their bounds.

codes.message: Application graph admission failed.
codes.recovery: Inspect the retained admission code and reduce the failing bound.

codes.message: Task callback failed.
codes.recovery: Handle the callback error before polling again.

codes.message: Wire is already declared.
codes.recovery: Remove the duplicate source-to-destination wire.

codes.message: A task cannot wire to itself.
codes.recovery: Choose two distinct endpoint tasks.

codes.message: Task role is unsupported.
codes.recovery: Choose periodic, control, or service.

codes.message: Board or app schema is unsupported.
codes.recovery: Choose a supported board and the current app schema.

codes.message: Application graph needs at least one task.
codes.recovery: Declare one bounded task before validation.

codes.message: Application declaration shape or field type is invalid.
codes.recovery: Correct the named field and remove unknown fields.
